overall_assessment.fls_score_context: Your 477 FLS score represents solid intermediate performance with excellent knot technique but room for improvement in speed and precision.
overall_assessment.session_headline: Excellent surgeon's knot execution with proper hand switching - focus next on placement accuracy and efficiency gains

rubric_insights.observation: 120-second completion time is respectable but leaves significant points on the table - you're losing ~123 points to time alone
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Time penalty of approximately 120 points from the 600-point maximum

rubric_insights.observation: Combined deviation of 2.7mm from both marks shows good but not excellent placement accuracy
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Lost 2.7 points due to placement deviation - minimal but perfectible

rubric_insights.observation: Outstanding knot technique with proper surgeon's knot, hand switching, and secure final construct
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Full points earned for knot quality - this is a major strength

technique_coaching.observation: In frame 2, suture appears positioned but with slight deviation from optimal mark placement - this suggests either initial placement inaccuracy or tissue distortion during passage
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Precise placement is critical for real surgical applications and represents easy points to capture
technique_coaching.correction: Focus on perpendicular needle entry at exact mark centers, using the mark as a bullseye rather than general target area
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 20 needle passes through paper targets with 2mm circles - aim for dead center entry and exit

technique_coaching.observation: Phase timing shows 35 seconds for initial suture placement - this suggests either hesitation or inefficient needle handling during the setup phase
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Efficient setup directly translates to faster overall times and better OR flow
technique_coaching.correction: Develop a consistent pre-planned approach: visualize both entry and exit points before first needle grasp
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 10 rapid suture placements focusing only on speed of initial pass - target under 20 seconds for placement phase

technique_coaching.observation: Excellent surgeon's knot formation in frames 4-6 with proper double throw technique and good instrument control throughout wrapping sequence
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: This technique is already at proficiency level - maintaining this quality while increasing speed is the goal
technique_coaching.correction: Continue current technique but work on smoother transitions between wraps to eliminate micro-pauses
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 15 surgeon's knots on a peg board focusing on continuous motion during wrapping - no stops between wraps

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Outstanding surgeon's knot execution in frames 4-6 with textbook double throw technique and controlled tensioning
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: This is advanced technique that many trainees struggle with - you've mastered the most challenging aspect of this task
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your knot quality is genuinely excellent - this level of technique will serve you well in actual surgery

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Proper hand switching between throws visible in frames 7 and 10, demonstrating good understanding of alternating tension principles
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Hand switching prevents knot slippage and is essential for secure knot construction
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your consistent hand switching shows you understand the biomechanics of secure knot tying

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Clean, controlled cutting technique in frames 13-14 with appropriate distance from knot and complete suture removal
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Proper cutting prevents knot loosening and ensures clean wound closure
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your cutting technique is precise and safe - no wasted motion or risk of knot damage

practice_plan.session_focus: Reduce suture placement time from 35s to under 20s while maintaining current excellent knot quality
practice_plan.warmup_drill: 5 minutes of rapid needle loading practice - focus on consistent 2/3 grasp point

primary_drill.name: Speed Placement Challenge
primary_drill.description: Set up 5 Penrose drains with marks. Time yourself placing suture through both marks on each drain. Focus on: 1) Pre-visualizing entry/exit, 2) Single smooth needle pass, 3) Immediate setup for knot tying. Reset and repeat.
primary_drill.success_criteria: Achieve placement in under 20 seconds with deviation under 2mm on 80% of attempts

practice_plan.cooldown: Review your fastest successful placement - what made it smooth? Note any hesitation points for next session.
practice_plan.estimated_sessions_to_improvement: 3-4 focused sessions should get you consistently under 100 seconds total time

progress_context.vs_baseline: Significant improvement from early attempts - you've mastered the fundamental techniques
progress_context.vs_peak: Current performance matches your recent best scores in the 508-509 range
progress_context.milestone_note: Next target: Sub-100 second completion with current knot quality for 550+ FLS score

coaching_limitations: Camera angle during initial suture placement (frame 2) makes it difficult to assess exact needle entry angle - recommendations based on timing and final position only
